title: verbascum humile subsp. juruk
intro: Verbascum humile subsp. juruk is a subspecies of the Verbascum genus, commonly known as mullein. It is a flowering plant native to certain regions, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers and woolly leaves. Verbascum humile subsp. juruk has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial for respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
